firm.name: FalkenSteg
firm.oneLiner: FalkenSteg is an independent advisory firm focused on corporate finance, restructuring, insolvency advisory, operations, and real estate.
fitNotes: Clear fit for deal workflow support in special situations, especially M&A and restructuring.
They explicitly run structured M&A processes and handle complex transactions across multiple stakeholders.
No data room or client portal is mentioned on the site, so workflow digitization is not evidenced.
Strong presence in insolvency and turnaround work suggests process-heavy deals where an AI-native platform could help.

people.name: Jonas Eckhardt

footprint.title: Perlon transaction quote
footprint.summary: Quoted on achieving a comprehensive solution for the Perlon Group and laying the basis for sustainable continuation of the business.

likelyPriorities: Faster buyer coordination across complex cross-border processes, inferred.
Better visibility into transaction status and stakeholder communication, inferred.

footprint.title: Himolla support

Faster scenario planning and restructuring execution, inferred.

signals.title: Perlon Group becomes part of Wuxi Yinda Nylon
Falkensteg supports Himolla in Schutzschirmverfahren
signals.detail: FalkenSteg announced support for the Himolla Group on 2026-06-26.

play.angle: FalkenSteg looks like a process-heavy advisory firm that already runs structured M&A and restructuring mandates, so lead with reducing coordination friction rather than pitching generic AI. The strongest hook is that their work appears email-based and partner-led, which makes a short demo of faster buyer management, stage-gated document release, and cleaner status visibility relevant. Avoid sounding like a data-room replacement pitch first, or anything that suggests they need to change how they advise clients, since they likely care most about control and professionalism.

objections.objection: We already manage deals well with email and our current tools.
objections.counter: That is exactly the workflow Fintalo is meant to sit on top of, not force a process change. The value is less manual follow-up, clearer status, and tighter control over who sees what, without disrupting how you run the mandate.

objections.objection: Our mandates are sensitive, especially in restructuring and insolvency.
objections.counter: Understood, and that is why the demo should focus on permissions, auditability, and controlled release rather than broad collaboration. If it does not feel safer than email, it is not a fit.

objections.objection: We do both M&A and restructuring, so one tool may not fit both.
objections.counter: Fintalo is built for exactly that mix, with buyer/investor workflows for transactions and stage-gated collaboration for sensitive processes. The point is to give partners one operating layer across both types of mandates.

openingEmail.subject: Quick idea for FalkenSteg
openingEmail.body:
Hi FalkenSteg team,

I saw that FalkenSteg was immediately mandated as exclusive M&A advisor after the filing. That kind of time-sensitive process is exactly where teams tend to lose time across email threads, document versions, and buyer follow-up.

Fintalo is an AI-native M&A operating system that helps advisors manage NDA workflows, buyer coordination, stage-gated document release, and deal status in one place. If useful, I would be glad to show you a short demo.

Best,
openingEmail.groundedFact: FalkenSteg was immediately mandated as exclusive M&A advisor after the filing.
